rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly states it “complements prior work on really new products” and addresses a closely related mechanism (improving evaluations of highly novel products via resolving/understanding benefits), which aligns strongly with the cited paper’s imagination-focused visualization and perceived-benefit-value findings. Given this direct conceptual overlap and exact DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.46.1.46), this is not clearly an incidental mention.
